quote: 1. The stock doesn't know you own it. You feel emotional about a stock, but the stock doesn't feel anything about you.
speaker: Peter Lynch

quote: 2. In this business, if you're good, you're right six times out of ten. You're never going to be right nine times out of ten.
speaker: Peter Lynch

quote: 3. Far more money has been lost by investors preparing for corrections, or trying to anticipate corrections, than has been lost in corrections themselves.
speaker: Peter Lynch

quote: 4. Know what you own, and know why you own it.
speaker: Peter Lynch

quote: 5. Go for a business that any idiot can run — because eventually, any idiot probably is going to run it.
speaker: Peter Lynch

quote: 6. Long shots almost never pay off.
speaker: Peter Lynch

quote: 7. You should be able to explain why you own a stock in such simple terms that a fifth grader could understand it.
speaker: Peter Lynch

quote: 8. Numbers are nice, but the basic story line is what matters.
speaker: Peter Lynch

quote: 9. Owning stocks is like having children — don't get involved with more than you can handle.
speaker: Peter Lynch

quote: 10. The best stock to buy may be the one you already own.
speaker: Peter Lynch

quote: 11. Investing without research is like playing stud poker and never looking at the cards.
speaker: Peter Lynch

quote: 12. Time is on your side when you own shares of superior companies.
speaker: Peter Lynch

quote: 13. In the long run, it's not just how much money you make that will determine your future prosperity. It's how much of that money you put to work by saving it and investing it.
speaker: Peter Lynch

quote: 14. Everyone has the brainpower to make money in stocks. Not everyone has the stomach.
speaker: Peter Lynch

quote: 15. Understanding the story of a stock you own or intend to buy is as basic as understanding why your kid needs a shot.
speaker: Peter Lynch

quote: 16. The person that turns over the most rocks wins the game.
speaker: Peter Lynch

quote: 17. Absent a lot of surprises, stocks are relatively predictable over twenty years. As to whether they're going to be higher or lower in two or three years, you might as well flip a coin to decide.
speaker: Peter Lynch

quote: 18. If you like the store, chances are you'll love the stock.
speaker: Peter Lynch

quote: 19. Never invest in any idea you can't illustrate with a crayon.
speaker: Peter Lynch

quote: 20. Selling your winners and holding your losers is like cutting the flowers and watering the weeds.
speaker: Peter Lynch

quote: 21. Gentlemen who prefer bonds don't know what they're missing.
speaker: Peter Lynch

quote: 22. When you sell in desperation, you always sell cheap.
speaker: Peter Lynch

quote: 23. Behind every stock is a company. Find out what it's doing.
speaker: Peter Lynch

quote: 24. All you need for a lifetime of successful investing is a few big winners, and the pluses from those will overwhelm the minuses from the stocks that don't work out.
speaker: Peter Lynch

quote: 25. Every once in a while, the market does something so stupid it takes your breath away.
speaker: Peter Lynch

quote: 26. There's no point in getting insight into a company if you don't stick around long enough to profit from it.
speaker: Peter Lynch

quote: 27. In the long run, a portfolio of well-chosen stocks and/or equity mutual funds will always outperform a portfolio of bonds or a money-market account.
speaker: Peter Lynch

quote: 28. You can't see the future through a rearview mirror.
speaker: Peter Lynch

quote: 29. Just because Warren Buffett is buying a stock doesn't mean you should buy it too.
speaker: Peter Lynch

quote: 30. There is always something to worry about. Avoid weekend thinking and ignore the latest dire predictions of newsletter writers.
speaker: Peter Lynch

quote: 31. A decline in the stock market is as routine as a January blizzard in Colorado.
speaker: Peter Lynch

quote: 32. If you can't convince yourself 'When I'm down 25 percent, I'm a buyer' and banish forever the fatal thought 'When I'm down 25 percent, I'm a seller,' then you'll never make a decent profit in stocks.
speaker: Peter Lynch

quote: 33. All the math you need in the stock market you get in the fourth grade.
speaker: Peter Lynch

quote: 34. Averaging down in a company that has fundamental problems is like adding water to a watered-down drink.
speaker: Peter Lynch

quote: 35. Companies that have no debt can't go bankrupt.
speaker: Peter Lynch

quote: 36. Just avoid the losers and the fear will take care of itself.
speaker: Peter Lynch

quote: 37. Big companies have small moves, small companies have big moves.
speaker: Peter Lynch

quote: 38. If a company uses a technology you don't understand, what it accomplishes, and how it can serve customers, don't invest in it.
speaker: Peter Lynch

quote: 39. Invest in the company with the fewest color photographs in the annual report.
speaker: Peter Lynch

quote: 40. I made most of my money out of the third and fourth year I owned something, not the first year.
speaker: Peter Lynch

quote: 41. Nobody can predict interest rates, the future direction of the economy, or the stock market. Dismiss all such forecasts and concentrate on what's actually happening to the companies in which you've invested.
speaker: Peter Lynch

quote: 42. There's no shame in losing money on a stock. Everybody does it. What is shameful is holding on to a stock, or worse, buying more of it, when the fundamentals are deteriorating.
speaker: Peter Lynch
